terms.definition: Regression that models y as a linear combination of polynomial powers of input features (1, x, x^2, ...), with coefficients fit by ordinary linear regression methods.
Derived input columns formed by raising original features to integer powers (and optionally interactions), e.g. x, x^2, x1*x2, used as inputs to a linear model.
The highest power d in a polynomial model; degree 1 is linear, higher degrees allow more curvature and more parameters.
Design matrix with columns [1, x, x^2, ..., x^d] for a single input x; the standard feature matrix for univariate polynomial regression.
A model where the target is a linear combination of fixed features and unknown coefficients; polynomial regression is non-linear in x but linear in parameters.
